diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatty-meal-triggered RUQ pain reflects CCK-driven gallbladder contraction against an obstructing stone — hallmark of biliary colic.
Same anatomical territory; postprandial RUQ pain is an early feature but cholecystitis adds inflammation/fever and sustained pain.
Upper-abdominal postprandial pain overlaps but PUD pain is usually epigastric and not referred to the right scapula.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain radiating to the right shoulder blade / scapula

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Referred pain to the right scapula/shoulder via shared T5-T9 and phrenic pathways is characteristic of gallbladder distension.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute myocardial infarction / acute coronary syndrome
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cardiac pain typically radiates retrosternally to the left arm, neck or jaw; strict RIGHT-scapular radiation points away from a cardiac source.

symptoms.symptom_name: Restlessness — pacing, unable to lie still or find a comfortable position

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Visceral colicky pain drives restlessness (the patient writhes/paces), in contrast to the stillness of peritoneal irritation.
Established peritoneal inflammation (cholecystitis/peritonitis) makes patients lie still to avoid movement; active restlessness favours colic over peritonism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nausea with two episodes of non-bilious vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nausea and vomiting commonly accompany a biliary colic attack.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute myocardial infarction / acute coronary syndrome
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nausea/vomiting is non-specific and can occur in inferior MI as well; does not discriminate on its own.
Absence of fever makes gallbladder-wall infection/inflammation less likely, favouring uncomplicated colic.
No fever/rigors means Charcot's triad is not met — cholangitis not currently indicated.
No jaundice argues against common-bile-duct obstruction with infection.
A patient-observed absence of jaundice lowers the probability of an obstructing CBD stone, though early obstruction can precede visible icterus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative Murphy's sign (no inspiratory arrest on RUQ palpation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative Murphy's sign lowers the likelihood of acute cholecystitis — but it is a clinician-elicited examination finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely; it is sealed and must not be assumed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No rebound tenderness or involuntary guarding (no peritonism)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of peritonism argues against a complicated/perforated process; a clinician-only palpation finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localised right-upper-quadrant tenderness on deep palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised RUQ tenderness confirms the gallbladder territory as the pain source; requires in-person palpation, so sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile with stable vital signs (Temp 36.8, HR 95, BP 140/85, RR 18, SpO2 98%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normothermia and haemodynamic stability argue against systemic infection/sepsis; these are clinician-measured vitals (no home device reported), so sealed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute myocardial infarction / acute coronary syndrome
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal SpO2 and haemodynamics are reassuring but do not exclude ACS without an ECG/troponin.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transabdominal ultrasound: mobile echogenic gallstones with acoustic shadowing, no gallbladder-wall thickening

investigation_details.investigation_name: Transabdominal ultrasound of the right upper quadrant
investigation_details.expected_result: Mobile echogenic gallstones with posterior acoustic shadowing; normal gallbladder wall (no thickening), no pericholecystic fluid, no CBD dilatation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without imaging the gallstones cannot be confirmed and evolving cholecystitis (wall thickening) cannot be excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mobile gallstones with a normal wall and settling pain confirm uncomplicated symptomatic cholelithiasis.
Absence of wall thickening / pericholecystic fluid argues strongly against acute cholecystitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG and troponin (to exclude a cardiac mimic)

investigation_details.expected_result: Expected normal / non-ischaemic, given the biliary picture
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Only warranted if the history raises cardiac suspicion; here it is a consider-if-doubt item rather than a routine order.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute myocardial infarction / acute coronary syndrome
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: If any cardiac doubt exists, an ECG/troponin is the only reliable way to exclude ACS — obtainable only in person.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Fatty-meal-triggered severe RUQ pain radiating to the right scapula, with restlessness and vomiting, is the classic uncomplicated biliary colic picture.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Reassuring negatives that exclude complicated biliary disease
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Absent fever/rigors and absent jaundice are the screening negatives that move the picture away from cholecystitis and cholangitis — and are exactly the safety-net triggers.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Transabdominal ultrasound of the right upper quadrant
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line imaging to confirm gallstones and, critically, to exclude gallbladder-wall thickening / pericholecystic fluid of acute cholecystitis and any CBD dilatation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods — FBC, CRP, liver function tests (bilirubin, ALP, GGT, ALT/AST) and lipase
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screens for the leucocytosis/raised CRP of cholecystitis, the obstructive LFT pattern of choledocholithiasis/cholangitis, and lipase for gallstone pancreatitis — none obtainable via telehealth.
Reasonable to exclude an atypical cardiac cause of upper-abdominal pain with diaphoresis if any doubt exists on in-person review; not mandatory given the strongly biliary picture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat / serial abdominal examination in person
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Murphy's sign, guarding and rebound cannot be assessed remotely; in-person examination is needed to exclude peritonism and evolving cholecystitis.
Not first-line for suspected uncomplicated biliary colic — ultrasound is the appropriate initial imaging; routine CT would be over-investigation and unnecessary radiation.
